policy: All Slither detector families emitted by the contracts audit-evidence workflow must be listed here. New detector families fail the triage gate until they are fixed, covered by regression tests, or explicitly accepted with rationale.

arbitrary-send-eth.rationale: Marketplace native payouts intentionally send ETH to dynamic sellers, royalty recipients, bidders, and fee recipients. Public write entrypoints remain nonReentrant and payout behavior is covered by regression tests.

low-level-calls.rationale: Native ETH transfer uses call and reverts on failure. This is intentional for marketplace payouts and refunds.

timestamp.rationale: Listings, offers, and auctions are time-bounded by design, so block.timestamp comparisons are expected.

reentrancy-events.rationale: Event-after-transfer warnings are informational for the current event semantics. Critical state is deactivated before settlement/payout paths, and event ordering is preserved for consumers.

reentrancy-no-eth.rationale: Auction custody creation is nonReentrant. Malicious ERC-721/ERC-1155 transfer mocks cannot create duplicate auction state during custody transfer.

reentrancy-benign.rationale: Offer escrow and auction creation paths are nonReentrant. Malicious ERC-20/ERC-721/ERC-1155 mocks cannot create duplicate state during external token callbacks.

dead-code.rationale: The split ERC-721 and ERC-1155 entrypoints share a centralized base for storage and helper discipline. Per-entrypoint analysis sees opposite-standard helpers as unused.

uninitialized-state.rationale: Per-entrypoint analysis sees opposite-standard mappings as uninitialized/unused. Those mappings are used by the other split entrypoint and by the legacy compatibility wrapper; centralized storage is intentional.

constable-states.rationale: Per-entrypoint analysis sees opposite-standard counters as constant candidates. They are mutable in the other split entrypoint and legacy compatibility wrapper.
